task.summary: Continue fixing /api/projects after Airtable returned 429 and D1 cache was empty.
task.constraint: CSV fallback must remain disabled for project cards.

selectedBundles.reason: Branch, trace, PR and CI governance.
ResearchOps Worker route and service contract.
Operational risk and public-service service assurance.
Cloudflare Worker, Pages preview and D1 resilience behaviour.
Airtable rate-limit handling and authoritative record-source contract.

evidence.summary: /api/projects returned 503 projects_unavailable with sources[0].status 429 and message airtable_http_429.
project-record-routes.js previously only populated D1 after successful Airtable reads and did not report an empty D1 cache as a source diagnostic.

implementation: Read D1 project cache first unless refresh=1 is requested.
Sync full Airtable project payloads into D1 after successful Airtable reads.
Return D1 projects with x-rops-source: d1 when cache is populated.
Expose Airtable failures as x-rops-upstream-warning when serving D1 fallback.
Return D1 empty-cache diagnostics when neither source can serve projects.
Allow /api/projects/:id to fall back to D1 when Airtable is unavailable or rate-limited.

validation.detail: npm run validate failed because trace coverage expected docs/agent-audit/reasoning/2026/05/17/.
Added 2026/05/17 trace markdown and JSON files.

residualRisks: If D1 is empty and Airtable remains rate-limited, the endpoint will still return 503 until Airtable succeeds or D1 is intentionally seeded from authoritative rec... records.
Further CI checks may reveal formatter, contract or unit-test expectations that need repair.
End-to-end browser proof is still required before claiming the Projects page is fixed.
